acceptance_targets: [chapter_05_award_scene_escalation] Pass if, on a reread of the breakfast sequence between snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:119 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:157, the scene introduces a consequence that is not already stated between snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:31 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:79, and the breakfast scene does not spend more than one paragraph re-defining how the substrate works. Fail if the second scene can still be summarized mainly as Gavin understanding agreements under spells rather than paying a new cost.

[chapter_05_craft_clipped_narration] Pass if every sentence in the revised narration at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:89, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:101, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:119, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:121, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:139 contains a grammatical subject and a finite verb unless it is quoted speech or notebook text. Fail if standalone noun-phrase or adjective-phrase fragments remain in narration for emphasis.

[chapter_05_prose_concept_token_overuse] Pass if a reader can track the revelation in the breakfast passage between snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:105 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:139 through concrete sensory changes and social consequence without adjacent paragraphs repeating the same concept nouns. Fail if neighboring paragraphs still restate agreement/rules/structure language without adding a new observable effect.

[chapter_05_prose_composed_register] Pass if the revised lines at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:81, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:83, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:125 read as observation under stress rather than as polished aphorism or narrator flourish. Fail if those spans still contain personified abstractions or a sentence that feels designed to be admired on its own.
[length_chapter_05_min_words] Chapter word count is >= 5000 while preserving heading contract.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if, on a reread of the breakfast sequence between snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:119 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:157, the scene introduces a consequence that is not already stated between snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:31 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:79, and the breakfast scene does not spend more than one paragraph re-defining how the substrate works. Fail if the second scene can still be summarized mainly as Gavin understanding agreements under spells rather than paying a new cost.
findings.problem: Both scenes spend major space defining the same revelation instead of escalating from concept to consequence. The sub-collection scene already establishes that the First Art is the rule-layer beneath spells and that Gavin has been altered; the breakfast scene then re-explains the agreement model at length instead of using the new perception to force a sharper social, bodily, or practical turn. That keeps the chapter below shortlist-level dramatic compression.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the conceptual passage anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:31 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:43, then revise the breakfast passage anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:119 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:157, so scene one owns the explanation and scene two owns the consequence. In the breakfast scene, trim repeated substrate-definition material and spend the recovered space on a new category of pressure: a clearer bodily overload, a stronger failed attempt to answer Miren, or another concrete disruption that only this perception could cause.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if every sentence in the revised narration at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:89, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:101, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:119, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:121, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:139 contains a grammatical subject and a finite verb unless it is quoted speech or notebook text. Fail if standalone noun-phrase or adjective-phrase fragments remain in narration for emphasis.
findings.problem: The narration repeatedly uses clipped fragments such as "Half-eleven by his watch.", "Empty.", "Toast.", "An agreement.", and "A rider." even though the constitution explicitly bans elliptical clipping in narration. The accumulation creates a mannered staccato and reads like authored emphasis rather than Gavin's lived thought.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rewrite the fragment-heavy narration anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:7, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:89, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:101, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:119, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:121, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:139 as grammatically complete sentences. Keep the pressure, but restore explicit subjects and verbs instead of dropping them for effect.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if a reader can track the revelation in the breakfast passage between snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:105 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:139 through concrete sensory changes and social consequence without adjacent paragraphs repeating the same concept nouns. Fail if neighboring paragraphs still restate agreement/rules/structure language without adding a new observable effect.
findings.problem: The chapter leans too hard on the same style-bible nouns and locutions: "agreement(s)", "rules", "structure", "beneath", and "underneath". Because those terms recur in both scenes without enough lexical variation or fresh sensory conversion, the revelation starts to feel paraphrased instead of discovered.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the explanatory passages anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:15, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:31, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:43, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:105, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:119, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:139 so the conceptual vocabulary appears only where naming is necessary. Elsewhere, let the substrate register through concrete effects already present in the chapter: temperature bands, tooth-pressure, wrist-slackening, table strain, Hall pressure, and missed social cues.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised lines at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:81, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:83, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:125 read as observation under stress rather than as polished aphorism or narrator flourish. Fail if those spans still contain personified abstractions or a sentence that feels designed to be admired on its own.
findings.problem: Several lines tip from close-third bewilderment into polished literary performance: the line about the person looking at his hands changing first, the comparison of his notes to evidence from a crime against his own mind, and the Hall answering the spoon's rebellion against gravity. The style bible allows strange perception, but not narrator-level eloquence that sounds more composed than Gavin.
findings.rewrite_direction: Recast the language anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:81, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:83, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:125 in plainer Gavin-shaped diction. Keep the uncanniness, but anchor it to schoolboy frames of reference and immediate bodily fact rather than quotable metaphoric finish.

findings.acceptance_test: Chapter word count is >= 5000 while preserving heading contract.
findings.rewrite_direction: Add causally necessary scene material that strengthens the chapter engine, increases pressure, and lands the planned state shift without filler.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This novel's central risk is tonal: it must sustain dark comedy, cosmic horror, and genuine emotional tenderness in the same narrative without any register canceling the others. Lean into the discomfort of juxtaposition. A scene where Gavin is mentally modeling ward-substrate decay while a classmate screams under the Cruciatus Curse is not a failure of empathy — it is the novel's core tension made visceral. The humor of the premise — that Voldemort's war is an irritating distraction from the real problem — must never be defused by making Gavin feel appropriately guilty at the right moments. He should feel guilty sometimes and not other times, and the reader should sit with the discomfort of a protagonist whose priorities have become genuinely alien. The cosmic material should be rendered with enough specificity that it is genuinely unsettling, not just philosophically interesting. Do not soften the ending: Gavin's refusal to intervene in the Battle of Hogwarts must be played straight, without a last-minute redemptive gesture or moral safety net.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism in this novel is a particularly acute failure because the entire thematic engine runs on the difference between seeing something directly and not seeing it. Gavin's journey is toward unmediated perception. The prose must mirror that commitment. When the Carrows torture a student, render the torture. When Gavin is hit with the Cruciatus, render the pain in his body. When the cosmic threat presses against perception, render what it feels like — do not retreat to abstractions about 'something vast' or 'an incomprehensible presence.' Find the specific sensory detail that makes the incomprehensible land. Euphemism in a novel about the failure to see what is real is thematic self-sabotage.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the norm for all dialogue. Uncontracted forms are reserved for deliberate emphasis, Babbling's academic register, or moments where a character is being pointedly formal. If a line reads stiffly, check contractions first.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate chapter textures aggressively. A high-intensity chapter (Cruciatus detention, Duncan's torture) must be followed by a chapter with a different pressure profile — quieter, more internal, more comic, or more focused on intellectual work. No two consecutive chapters should share the same dominant emotional register, the same scene-structure (e.g., two chapters that each build to a confrontation), or the same pacing profile. Vary scene count, dialogue density, and the ratio of interiority to action. Some chapters should be mostly one extended scene; others should move across multiple locations and timeframes. The Christmas chapter and the work-routine chapters should breathe differently from the Carrow chapters and the battle chapter.
prose_style_profile.diction: Plain, concrete, specific. Anglo-Saxon roots preferred over Latinate where both are available. Avoid poetic diction, literary allusion, and metaphors that call attention to their own cleverness. Metaphors should arise from Gavin's actual frame of reference: schoolwork, the castle, ordinary physical experience. When the First Art requires description, reach for precision over beauty — describe what it looks like, not what it means. The novel's most striking language should come from the collision between ordinary diction and extraordinary perception.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, locked to Gavin's perception throughout. The narrative voice has access to his thoughts, feelings, and sensory experience but not to anyone else's interiority. Other characters are known only through what Gavin observes. As the First Art restructures Gavin's cognition, the narrative's perceptual range should gradually expand — he begins noticing things no one else can see — but the voice remains grounded in his ordinary, slightly bewildered perspective. The narration should never become more articulate or literary than Gavin himself would be; the strangeness of what he perceives should press against the ordinariness of how he describes it.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Varied and responsive to scene pressure. Default rhythm is medium-paced prose with sentences of mixed length — some short, some compound, none habitually long. During scenes of intellectual discovery, let sentences extend as Gavin's attention extends, then cut short when comprehension fails. During Carrow scenes, prose should tighten and become more physical. During quiet scenes, allow room for observation and sensory detail without rushing toward the next event. Do not develop a signature rhythm that persists across all chapters — the rhythm should be the scene's rhythm, not the author's.
